factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with some segments experiencing decreases while others see increases.

evidence.text: The 7.0 and 4.2 percent decreases in net sales for Flow in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by decreased sales volume in our residential flow and industrial solutions businesses compared to the prior year. These decreases were partially offset by increased sales volume in our commercial flow business compared to the prior year; and increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases; and favorable foreign currency effects compared to the third quarter of the prior year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed with some segments decreasing and others increasing.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

evidence.text: The 1.5 and 0.3 percent decreases in net sales in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by decreased sales volume in our residential flow and industrial solutions businesses within our Flow segment compared to the prior year; decreased sales volume within our commercial and residential businesses in our Water Solutions segment compared to the prior year, in addition to the completion of a large project in 2023 within our commercial business that did not recur in the first nine months of 2024; and a product line exit in our Pool segment that occurred in the first nine months of 2024. These decreases were partially offset by increased selling prices in our Flow segment, in our commercial business within our Water Solutions segment and in our Pool segment to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Pool segment, while some lower-margin areas are being exited.

evidence.text: The 7.3 and 7.4 percent increases in net sales for Pool in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by increased sales volume due to higher demand compared to the prior year; and increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases. These increases were partially offset by a product line exit that occurred in the first nine months of 2024.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, indicating a strategic positive development.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed across segments, with some segments experiencing growth while others face declines.

evidence.text: The 7.3 and 7.4 percent increases in net sales for Pool in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by increased sales volume due to higher demand compared to the prior year; and increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases. These increases were partially offset by a product line exit that occurred in the first nine months of 2024.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is mixed across segments, with some growing and others declining.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in some areas but increasing in others, with overall demand mixed.

evidence.text: The 7.0 and 4.2 percent decreases in net sales for Flow in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by decreased sales volume in our residential flow and industrial solutions businesses compared to the prior year. These decreases were partially offset by increased sales volume in our commercial flow business compared to the prior year; and increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases; and favorable foreign currency effects compared to the third quarter of the prior year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining in some areas but increasing in others, indicating mixed demand.

factors.summary: End market momentum varies, with the Pool segment experiencing strong demand while other segments face challenges.

evidence.text: The 7.3 and 7.4 percent increases in net sales for Pool in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by increased sales volume due to higher demand compared to the prior year; and increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases. These increases were partially offset by a product line exit that occurred in the first nine months of 2024.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The Pool segment is experiencing strong demand, providing a positive momentum despite challenges in other segments.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, particularly in raw materials and labor.

evidence.text: The current volatile market for commodities has the potential to continue to drive price increases in our supply chain. While we have taken pricing actions and implemented transformation initiatives that we expect to improve productivity and offset cost increases, we anticipate inflationary cost increases to continue for the remainder of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, creating cost headwinds.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure.

evidence.text: The 2.7 and 2.3 percentage point increases in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflationary costs; and increased productivity mainly driven by transformation initiatives across all segments and manufacturing leverage within our Pool segment; and asset impairment and write-offs of $3.5 million recorded in the first nine months of 2024, compared to $5.3 million in the first nine months of 2023. These increases were partially offset by inflationary cost increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to pricing and productivity improvements, though inflation remains a headwind.

evidence.text: The 2.7 and 2.3 percentage point increases in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflationary costs; and increased productivity mainly driven by transformation initiatives across all segments and manufacturing leverage within our Pool segment; and asset impairment and write-offs of $3.5 million recorded in the first nine months of 2024, compared to $5.3 million in the first nine months of 2023. These increases were partially offset by inflationary cost increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to pricing and productivity improvements, indicating positive developments despite inflationary pressures.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives, including transformation programs, are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: During 2023 and the first nine months of 2024, we executed certain business restructuring initiatives aimed at reducing our fixed cost structure and realigning our business. We expect these actions to continue throughout the remainder of 2024 and to drive margin growth. The 28.0 and 20.0 percent decreases in net interest expense in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by lower variable debt compared to the same period of the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, with clear evidence of margin growth and cost structure improvements.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to transformation initiatives and operational efficiency gains.

evidence.text: The 2.7 and 2.3 percentage point increases in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2024 from 2023 were primarily driven by increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflationary costs; and increased productivity mainly driven by transformation initiatives across all segments and manufacturing leverage within our Pool segment; and asset impairment and write-offs of $3.5 million recorded in the first nine months of 2024, compared to $5.3 million in the first nine months of 2023. These increases were partially offset by inflationary cost increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements driven by transformation initiatives and operational efficiency gains support a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, such as the OECD Pillar Two Model Rules, are impacting the effective tax rate.

evidence.text: The Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development Pillar Two Model Rules ('Pillar Two') for a global 15.0% minimum tax are in the process of being adopted by a number of jurisdictions in which we operate. Pillar Two has negatively impacted our effective tax rate in 2024. That impact could change in the future as we continue to evaluate the enacted legislative changes and as new guidance becomes available.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes like the OECD Pillar Two Model Rules have negatively impacted the effective tax rate, indicating a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are not currently creating significant compliance costs or operational constraints.

evidence.text: We are composed of three reporting segments: Flow, Water Solutions and Pool. For the first nine months of 2024, the Flow, Water Solutions and Pool segments represented approximately 37%, 28% and 35% of total revenues, respectively. We classify our operations into reporting segments based primarily on types of products offered and markets served: • Flow — The focus of this segment is to deliver water where it is needed, when it is needed, more efficiently and to transform waste into value.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Environmental regulations are not currently creating significant compliance costs or operational constraints, leading to a neutral assessment.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by buyback commitments and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: We expect to execute these objectives by: • Delivering profitable revenue growth and productivity for customers and shareholders; • Continuing to focus on capital allocation through: ◦ Committing to maintain our investment grade rating; ◦ Focusing on reducing our long-term debt; ◦ Returning cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases; and ◦ Accelerating our performance with strategically aligned mergers and acquisitions; • Focusing growth initiatives that accelerate our investments in digital, innovation, technology and ESG; • Continuing to implement our Transformation Program initiatives that will drive operational excellence, reduce complexity and improve our organizational structure, which includes the focus on 80/20 actions to drive profitable growth; and • Building a high performance growth culture and delivering on our commitments while living our Win Right values.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence is evident through buyback commitments and capital expenditures, indicating a very positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing inflationary pressures and economic conditions impacting demand.

evidence.text: The current volatile market for commodities has the potential to continue to drive price increases in our supply chain. While we have taken pricing actions and implemented transformation initiatives that we expect to improve productivity and offset cost increases, we anticipate inflationary cost increases to continue for the remainder of 2024. The overall global economic and business conditions impacting our business, including the strength of housing and related markets and conditions relating to international hostilities; supply, demand, logistics, competition and pricing pressures related to and in the markets we serve; the ability to achieve the benefits of our restructuring plans, cost reduction initiatives and Transformation Program; the impact of raw material, logistics and labor costs and other inflation; volatility in currency exchange rates and interest rates; failure of markets to accept new product introductions and enhancements; the ability to successfully identify, finance, complete and integrate acquisitions; risks associated with operating foreign businesses; the impact of seasonality of sales and weather conditions; our ability to comply with laws and regulations; the impact of changes in laws, regulations and administrative policy, including those that limit U.S. tax benefits or impact trade agreements and tariffs; the outcome of litigation and governmental proceedings; and the ability to achieve our long-term strategic operating and environmental, social and governance ('ESG') goals and targets.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches and cost savings milestones.

evidence.text: We have identified specific product and geographic market opportunities that we find attractive and continue to pursue, both within and outside the U.S. We expect to continue investing in our businesses to drive these opportunities through research and development and additional sales and marketing resources. Unless we successfully penetrate these markets, our core sales growth will likely be limited or may decline. In 2024, our operating objectives focus on delivering our core and building our future. We expect to execute these objectives by: • Delivering profitable revenue growth and productivity for customers and shareholders; • Continuing to focus on capital allocation through: ◦ Committing to maintain our investment grade rating; ◦ Focusing on reducing our long-term debt; ◦ Returning cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases; and ◦ Accelerating our performance with strategically aligned mergers and acquisitions; • Focusing growth initiatives that accelerate our investments in digital, innovation, technology and ESG; • Continuing to implement our Transformation Program initiatives that will drive operational excellence, reduce complexity and improve our organizational structure, which includes the focus on 80/20 actions to drive profitable growth; and • Building a high performance growth culture and delivering on our commitments while living our Win Right values.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include technology and brand strength.

evidence.text: At Pentair, we believe the health of our world depends on reliable access to clean water. We deliver a comprehensive range of smart, sustainable water solutions to homes, businesses and industries around the world. Our industry-leading and proven portfolio of solutions enables our customers to access clean, safe water; reduce water consumption; and recover and reuse water. Whether it’s moving, improving or helping people enjoy water, we help manage life’s most essential resource. We are composed of three reporting segments: Flow, Water Solutions and Pool. For the first nine months of 2024, the Flow, Water Solutions and Pool segments represented approximately 37%, 28% and 35% of total revenues, respectively. We classify our operations into reporting segments based primarily on types of products offered and markets served: • Flow — The focus of this segment is to deliver water where it is needed, when it is needed, more efficiently and to transform waste into value.
